events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario: timing marks misaligned by a tooth on an automotive powertrain timing system. This is a clear fail - timing marks being off by a tooth means the timing is not within tolerance. This could cause engine damage, valve/piston contact, poor performance, etc.

Let me construct the JSON response.

- decision: fail (non-compliant, dangerous)
- risk: high or critical (timing misalignment can cause catastrophic engine damage)
- work_stage: rough-complete
- component_conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant
- percent_complete: maybe 70-80% since roughed in but not closed out
- value: 1 (one tooth misalignment)
- confidence: high, maybe 0.9
- findings: timing marks misaligned by one tooth
- actions: re-time the engine, realign timing marks per OEM spec
- rationale: timing marks must align exactly per OEM spec; one tooth off is out of tolerance and can cause valve-to-piston contact or poor engine performance
- references: OEM service info

events.label: response
events.text:
```json{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 75,
  "value": 1,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.9,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Timing marks misaligned by one tooth — crankshaft/camshaft synchronization out of specification",
    "Risk of valve-to-piston contact or incorrect valve timing if engine is rotated or started"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Do not rotate engine or start vehicle until timing is corrected",
    "Re-time engine per OEM service procedure: align all timing marks to specified positions",
    "Re-inspect timing mark alignment after correction before closing out"
  ],
  "rationale": "OEM service information requires exact alignment of crankshaft and camshaft timing marks. A one-tooth misalignment places valve timing outside tolerance and can cause catastrophic engine damage if the engine is rotated or started. The condition is non-compliant at the rough-complete stage and must be reworked before closure.",
  "references": ["OEM service info"]
}
```
